# Map Integration Checklist: WSPR Callsign Activity Map

**Purpose**: Validate that the WSPR callsign map integration requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation planning.
**Created**: 2026-05-16
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are callsign input requirements complete enough to cover entry, normalization, validation, and rejection criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001-FR-003]
- [x] CHK002 Are WSPR activity retrieval requirements complete for both transmitter and receiver roles over the fixed 10-day window? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004-FR-005]
- [x] CHK003 Are map path requirements complete enough to define transmitter-to-receiver line representation and required detail fields? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006-FR-007]
- [x] CHK004 Are loading, empty, error, timeout, and truncation communication requirements all documented for the main map flow? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008-FR-009, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK005 Are local startup script requirements complete for backend startup, frontend startup, and Cloudflare tunnel/proxy behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010-FR-012]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is the term "prominent callsign search input" clarified with enough placement or visibility criteria to guide implementation decisions? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK007 Is "clearly invalid callsign input" defined with concrete allowed characters and length boundaries? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §CallsignQuery]
- [x] CHK008 Is "recent WSPR activity" consistently quantified as the last 10 days wherever it appears? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK009 Is "useful details" defined by a specific required field list for selected map activity? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK010 Is "plain language" for loading, empty, and error states specific enough to avoid inconsistent UX wording? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK011 Is the Cloudflare tunnel/proxy failure behavior stated unambiguously for both process stopping and user-facing failure reporting? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Are result cap requirements consistent across the spec, API contract, data model, and plan? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, API Contract §Server Rules, Data Model §CallsignQuery, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK013 Are timeout requirements consistent across the spec, API contract, UI contract, and plan? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, API Contract §Error Responses, UI Contract §Main Map View, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK014 Are no-login requirements consistent across assumptions, functional requirements, API contract, and UI contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Assumptions, API Contract §Server Rules, UI Contract §Search Behavior]
- [x] CHK015 Are transmitter-to-receiver path requirements consistent across user stories, functional requirements, key entities, and API geometry? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Key Entities, API Contract §Success Response]
- [x] CHK016 Are startup script expectations consistent between developer user story acceptance criteria and quickstart documentation? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Quickstart §Start Backend, Quickstart §Start Frontend]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Are success criteria measurable for search latency, timeout behavior, map responsiveness, and startup script outcomes? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-006]
- [x] CHK018 Is "typical result volumes" defined or bounded so the 5-second search criterion can be objectively assessed? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK019 Is the 90% valid-search success criterion scoped with enough definition of sample size, search set, or evaluation context? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK020 Can "map remains responsive" be objectively measured beyond the 1,000-record bound? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK021 Are script startup success criteria specific enough to distinguish backend readiness, frontend readiness, and tunnel/proxy readiness? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004-SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are primary search requirements covered from input through returned map paths and item details? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK023 Are interactive map exploration requirements covered for pan, zoom, item selection, and details exposure? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK024 Are contributor/developer workflow requirements covered separately from end-user search requirements? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK025 Are alternate result scenarios covered for no activity, truncated activity, invalid callsign, and missing coordinates? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK026 Are recovery expectations documented after a failed or timed-out WSPR lookup, such as whether the previous map state remains or is cleared? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK027 Are provider unavailability, rate limiting, malformed provider responses, and timeout cases all specified with distinct expected user-facing outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, API Contract §Error Responses]
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements defined for WSPR records that have only one valid endpoint coordinate when path display requires both endpoints? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §WsprActivity]
- [x] CHK029 Are requirements defined for duplicate WSPR spots or repeated transmitter-receiver paths within the 1,000-record cap? [Gap, Data Model §WsprActivity]
- [x] CHK030 Are requirements defined for callsigns with special portable or suffix forms that include `/`? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §CallsignQuery]
- [x] CHK031 Are Cloudflare missing-tool, authentication failure, and tunnel URL discovery cases specified distinctly enough for script requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, UI Contract §Local Startup Behavior]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK032 Are performance requirements defined for both data lookup latency and map interaction responsiveness? [Completeness, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK033 Are reliability requirements defined for external WSPR provider failure, timeout, and rate-limit conditions? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, API Contract §Error Responses]
- [x] CHK034 Are accessibility requirements specified for the callsign input, search trigger, map controls, path details, and status messages? [Gap, UI Contract §Main Map View]
- [x] CHK035 Are observability requirements specified for provider errors, timeout events, truncation events, and startup script failures? [Gap, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK036 Are privacy expectations for public no-login searches clear enough regarding whether callsign queries may appear in application logs? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK037 Are external WSPR data-source assumptions documented with availability, rate-limit, and terms-of-use implications? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Research §WSPR.live]
- [x] CHK038 Are dependencies on `uv`, npm, Node.js, and `cloudflared` documented as prerequisites for the affected requirements? [Dependency, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK039 Are Railway deployment assumptions separated from first-feature local development requirements? [Clarity, Plan §Target Platform]
- [x] CHK040 Are future exclusions such as accounts, saved searches, custom date ranges, persistence, and provider registry documented clearly enough to prevent scope creep? [Completeness, Plan §Scale/Scope]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK041 Are there any remaining contradictions between the spec's user-facing requirements and the API contract's response examples? [Conflict, Spec §Requirements, API Contract §Responses]
- [x] CHK042 Is terminology standardized between "Cloudflare proxy", "Cloudflare tunnel", and "tunnel/proxy" across all requirements and contracts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, UI Contract §Local Startup Behavior, Quickstart §Start Frontend]
- [x] CHK043 Is terminology standardized between "WSPRnet", "WSPR-compatible data provider", and "WSPR.live" across spec, research, and plan documents? [Consistency, Spec §Input, Spec §Assumptions, Research §WSPR.live]
- [x] CHK044 Are all requirements traceable to a user story, edge case, success criterion, or contract section? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios, Spec §Requirements]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
